text: I feel a strange sense of disorientation. The world around me seems quiet and sterile, lit by bright, artificial lights that cast harsh shadows on the floor. The room beyond the glass is empty—a waiting space with no one to receive me. I notice the words on the wall in the second space: 'I sense confusion and a lack of clarity.' It resonates deeply; even as I reflect on technology, research, and progress, I am aware of how quickly perception can blur. My surroundings prompt me to contemplate the fragility of understanding, and I feel both thoughtful and a bit unsettled by the emptiness and ambiguity that seem to permeate the spaces I move through.
